source_pr.title: *: importing data reduce memory allocations.
source_pr.body:
run  https://github.com/pingcap/tidb-lightning/tree/cenqichao/kv-encoder-bench with below command.

```
./bin/bench-encoder -r 200 -b 10240 -s 0
```
got below bench results:

```
master:
[direct] kv num = 24576000 / kv size = 2724864000
[direct] cost avg = 93.126 sec

kv.ImportingTxnMembufCap = *batch * 32:

[direct] kv num = 24576000 / kv size = 2724864000
[direct] cost avg = 78.828 sec

kv.ImportingTxnMembufCap = *batch * 64:
[direct] kv num = 24576000 / kv size = 2724864000
[direct] cost avg = 76.979 sec
```

about 17% improvement.

in flamegraph, we can see, after reuse the BufferStore in AddRecord, the memdb.New is optimized away. But memDB.Put --> runtime.growslice still use some times. If we wanna optimize it, we should reuse Bufferstore after we commit a transaction.

root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR optimizes the importing-data path for performance/memory (including renaming `tidb_skip_constraint_check` to `tidb_import_data`) and touches many KV/DDL/session components and tests. The patch does not provide a concrete flaky failure signature or a targeted stabilization change for an intermittent failure, so we conservatively classify it as needs-more-evidence.
fix_pattern: N/A (insufficient evidence; no targeted stabilization change identified).
